answer_correctness.justification: The answer covers all key facts from the ground truth: SQL1 defined in 1986 and adopted by ISO in 1987, SQL2 adopted in 1992 as the formal standard for relational databases, and SQL3 in 1999 extending the standard with object-oriented features, user-defined data types, triggers, and user-defined functions. No contradictions present.
